(B) 15$$min
(C) 30$$min
(D) 45$$min

content.problem_latex:
A party game played with a six---sided dice is fair if the chance of winning is equal to  the chance of losing each time the dice is rolled. Which one of these games is fair?
(A) You win if you roll a 6.
(B) You win if you roll a 2 or a 5.
(C) You win if you roll a number greater than 4.
(D) You win if you roll a number less than 3.
(E) You win if you roll an odd number.
content.problem_plain: A party game played with a six---sided dice is fair if the chance of winning is equal to the chance of losing each time the dice is rolled. Which one of these games is fair? (A) You win if you roll a 6. (B) You win if you roll a 2 or a 5. (C) You win if you roll a number greater than 4. (D) You win if you roll a number less than 3. (E) You win if you roll an odd number.

answer.final_raw: You win if you roll an odd number.
answer.final_normalized: You win if you roll an odd number.
candidates: You win if you roll an odd number.

content.problem_latex:
Which of the following statements about a histogram is false?
(A) A histogram is a bar graph.
(B) A histogram can have an interval where the frequency or count is 0.
(C) Given a data set, the histogram we can make from this data set is unique.
(D) The sizes of all intervals in a histogram must be the same.

Three fund managers invested in stocks. Jack bought 66 stocks, Wendy bought 40, and Leo bought 23. Jack and Wendy both bought 17 of the same stocks, Wendy and Leo bought 13 of the same, and Leo and Jack bought 9 of the same. (A) 90
(B) 84
(C) 95
(D) 96

search.problem_text: Three fund managers invested in stocks. Jack bought 66 stocks, Wendy bought 40, and Leo bought 23. Jack and Wendy both bought 17 of the same stocks, Wendy and Leo bought 13 of the same, and Leo and Jack bought 9 of the same. All three of them bought 6 of the same stocks.~ How many stocks in total did the three managers purchase? (A) 90 (B) 84 (C) 95 (D) 96
